detaileddescription.para: True if the return value is bad: either returning a failure, or the DataIndex or FieldIndex is out of range. However, this is a node-local funtion so it can't report the FieldIndex status in all cases.
Returns data entry for this object

memberdef.name: eref
memberdef.referencedby: SetGet5< A1, A2, A3, A4, A5 >::set
SetGet6< A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6 >::set

detaileddescription.para: True if the data is present on the current node. Always true for globals, which confuses the matter somewhat.

memberdef.name: isGlobal

briefdescription.para: Returns true if the Element is global.

memberdef.referencedby: SetGet5< A1, A2, A3, A4, A5 >::set
SetGet6< A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6 >::set

memberdef.name: isOffNode

briefdescription.para: Returns true if we need to go off-node for calling operations.

memberdef.name: ObjId
memberdef.referencedby: SetGet5< A1, A2, A3, A4, A5 >::set
SetGet6< A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6 >::set

detaileddescription.para: Creates an ObjId by reading the path string Returns bad on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (Id i, unsigned int d=0, unsigned int f=0)
memberdef.name: ObjId

detaileddescription.para: Creates a ObjId using specified Id and DataIndex
Returns the absolute path including all array indices.
This class manages lookups for specific data entries in elements, in a node-independent manner. It is basically a composite of Id and DataIndex.
